title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_59 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: At p = 59 the earlier low-barrier pattern is no longer the right arithmetic regime: a zero-spill choice B = {1,163,167,173} works cleanly, A can stay on a fixed small palette, and C can be filled by the complementary 58-prime pool. This points to a cleaner high-barrier / complementary-prime-pool slice rather than another bounded-spill patch.
candidate_theorem_slice: A zero-spill high-barrier sufficient-condition slice for Γ(Z_p × Z_25): if {1,...,5p+19} contains three pairwise-coprime labels above (5p+19)/2 and at least p - 1 complementary primes after excluding a fixed small palette and those barrier primes, then take B = {1,b_1,b_2,b_3}, A from the small palette, C from the complementary prime pool, and D as the residual complement.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_59 × Z_25) survives skeptical verification, and this feeder materially upgrades the active F25 campaign because it exhibits a new zero-spill barrier regime rather than just one more bounded-spill continuation. The result is still unformalized feeder evidence, not an exact Lean-backed theorem or paper-ready family claim.
next_action: Run generalize on the F25 campaign using this verified p = 59 zero-spill feeder, then test z61-z25 or prove the complementary-prime-pool supply lemma before any instance-only Lean work.
